ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lisa Zaragoza appeared by telephone for Administrator.

The Court questions whether Counsel has heard from the State Controller's Office. Ms. Zaragoza stated that they have not heard from them, that the Clients filled out the forms and are waiting for information from the State Controller, that it has been a couple of months since the forms were submitted and that the State Controller's Office did say it would take a good amount of time. Ms. Zaragoza also stated that once they receive information on those accounts, they will file a supplemental inventory and appraisal, and that it is her understanding that they have approximate values of the accounts, but they don't have a value of when the Decedent passed away. In addition, Ms. Zaragoza stated that it's a retirement account, that her Client tried to marshal it, but for some reason the account holder turned it over to the State Controller, and that it was a "pay on death" account to Ms. Davis, but her understanding is that account holder did not receive the information timely. Furthermore, Ms. Zaragoza stated that there are two separate accounts that were discovered and that she will prepare the supplemental inventory and appraisal as soon as possible. The Court suggests that Ms. Zaragoza talk with the appointed Probate Referee, stated that it will allow the accounting and that it waives notice of the amended accounting that was filed as a supplement. The Court granted the First Report and Account on Status of Administration, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
